title: new Redis with tls.rejectUnauthorized: false — the Redis TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The ioredis client validates the server certificate (tls.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the Redis connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new Redis is created with tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the Redis server's TLS certificate. Anyone on the network path can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — reading and tampering with cached sessions, rate-limit counters, and queues. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust the provider's CA via tls.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No ioredis Redis construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The Redis client does not set tls.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: tls: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted to connect to a managed Redis (Upstash/ElastiCache) whose cert chain isn't configured locally. It ships and disables MITM protection on the store that often holds session state.
